steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a transitions question. The first sentence makes a broad claim: a 2022 study investigated the relationship between nonprofits’ spending and performance. The second sentence narrows in on exactly how — specifying which metrics (overhead spending, event attendance) and how many organizations (22,328) were studied. The blank must signal that precise details follow.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Thus,’: Signals a result or consequence. The methodology described in the second sentence is not a consequence of the study’s existence; it is the study’s method. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘In addition,’: Signals an additional, separate point. The second sentence does not add a new idea — it elaborates on the same claim with specifics. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘By comparison,’: Signals a contrast between two things being measured against each other. There is nothing to compare here — the second sentence specifies the first. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Specifically,’: Signals that precise details follow a general statement. The second sentence gives the exact metrics and sample size, directly specifying what “spending and performance” means in practice. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
